name: Advanced Analytics V1

goal.answer: Create a purpose-built TradeOutcomeEntity collection to replace the buggy ReportEntity, migrate existing historical data from ReportEntity, then implement self-contained V1 portfolio analytics endpoints: portfolio summary, asset allocation, performance metrics, and XIRR. TradeOutcomeEntity will correctly handle corporate actions (bonus, split, demerger) and multipart sells via dual cost basis tracking and sourceSellTransactionId linkage.

criteria.answer:
TradeOutcomeEntity exists with dual cost basis, charges, holdingPeriodDays, capitalGainsType, sourceSellTransactionId, isCaDerived
One-time migration/backfill script created that reads ReportEntity and populates TradeOutcomeEntity with best-effort field mapping; existing user data preserved
ReportEntity and all report-related classes deleted; PortfolioService writes to TradeOutcomeService instead
GET /analytics/user/{email}/portfolio-summary returns totalInvested, totalRealizedProfit, totalTrades, buyTrades, sellTrades, holdingCount
GET /analytics/user/{email}/asset-allocation returns investedValue and percentage per AssetType summing to 100
GET /analytics/user/{email}/performance-metrics returns win/loss counts, avg profit/loss per trade, winLossRatio, avg holding period, portfolio turnover, best/worst stock
POST /analytics/user/{email}/xirr returns xirr, xirrPercentage, cashFlowsCount, converged flag; supports optional currentPrices for open positions
XIRR Calculator matches Excel/Google Sheets XIRR for known test cases within 0.01% tolerance
AnalyticsService unit tests pass for all 4 features
AnalyticsIntegrationTest passes with seeded data including corporate actions and multipart sells

constraints.answer: No external market-price APIs for V1, No sector allocation or benchmarking, No pre-computed analytics snapshots or caching layer, Follow existing Spring Boot / MongoDB / Lombok conventions, Handle empty portfolios and edge cases gracefully

phases.answer: TradeOutcome Foundation + Migration, Analytics Skeleton, Portfolio Summary + Asset Allocation, Performance Metrics, XIRR Calculator + Endpoint, Tests

assumptions.answer: Research docs in .kimchi/docs already analyzed corporate action impact, multipart sell scenarios, and reports collection bugs; we use those conclusions directly; TradeOutcomeEntity will be populated during the existing sellStock() write path in PortfolioService, replacing ReportEntity entirely; XIRR must always be computed from TransactionEntity (not TradeOutcomeEntity) to preserve actual cash flows; MongoDB additive schema changes are backward-compatible if needed later; no migration script required for new fields; Multipart sells produce multiple TradeOutcomeEntity records per sell transaction, linked by sourceSellTransactionId for aggregation; Corporate actions (bonus, split, demerger) are reflected in TradeOutcomeEntity via caAdjustedBuyPrice and isCaDerived flag

phases.name: TradeOutcome Foundation + Migration
phases.goal: Replace buggy ReportEntity with clean TradeOutcomeEntity and preserve existing user data
phases.description: Create TradeOutcomeEntity with dual cost basis (originalBuyPrice + caAdjustedBuyPrice), full charge pro-rating, holdingPeriodDays, capitalGainsType, financialYear, sourceSellTransactionId, sourceBuyLotId, netProfit, profitPercentage, isCaDerived flag. Create TradeOutcomeContext, TradeOutcomeRepository, TradeOutcomeService. Build a one-time backfill migration that reads existing ReportEntity documents and creates corresponding TradeOutcomeEntity records with best-effort field population (missing fields like charges and netProfit are left null or computed where possible). Replace all ReportEntity write/read usage in PortfolioService with TradeOutcomeEntity. Delete ReportEntity, ReportContext, ReportService, ReportRepository, and ReportController.

steps.description: Create TradeOutcomeEntity, TradeOutcomeContext, TradeOutcomeRepository, TradeOutcomeService with full schema
Build one-time migration/backfill that reads existing ReportEntity and populates TradeOutcomeEntity with available fields
Replace reportService usage in PortfolioService sellStock() and clearAllRecordsForCustomer() with tradeOutcomeService
Delete ReportEntity, ReportContext, ReportService, ReportRepository, ReportController and verify clean compile

phases.name: Analytics Skeleton
phases.goal: Establish the API contract for analytics
phases.description: Create AnalyticsController with 4 endpoints, all request/response DTOs, and AnalyticsService skeleton returning stub data. Defines the public API contract.

steps.description: Create AnalyticsController with GET /portfolio-summary, GET /asset-allocation, GET /performance-metrics, POST /xirr
Create DTOs: PortfolioSummaryResponse, AssetAllocationResponse, PerformanceMetricsResponse, XirrRequest, XirrResponse, CashFlow
Create AnalyticsService skeleton with stub methods

phases.name: Portfolio Summary + Asset Allocation
phases.description: Implement getPortfolioSummary() from AssetEntity + ProfitAndLossEntity + TransactionEntity. Implement getAssetAllocation() grouping AssetEntity by AssetType with percentage computation.

steps.description: Implement getPortfolioSummary() computing totalInvested, totalRealizedProfit, trade counts, holdingCount
Implement getAssetAllocation() with investedValue and percentage per AssetType

phases.name: Performance Metrics
phases.description: Implement getPerformanceMetrics() using TradeOutcomeEntity. Compute win/loss counts, average profit/loss per trade, win/loss ratio, average holding period, portfolio turnover, and best/worst stock by realized return. Use sourceSellTransactionId to optionally group by sell transaction. Handle migrated records gracefully where some pre-computed fields may be null.

steps.description: Implement performance metrics aggregations from TradeOutcomeEntity with null-safe handling for migrated records
Implement best/worst stock selection and portfolio turnover
Handle edge cases: empty data, all wins, all losses, zero turnover, null fields in migrated records

phases.name: XIRR Calculator + Endpoint
phases.goal: Deliver accurate XIRR using actual cash flows
phases.description: Build pure Java XirrCalculator using Newton-Raphson. Implement AnalyticsService.calculateXirr() constructing cash flows from TransactionEntity (BUY negative, SELL positive, optional notional inflow for open positions from request body currentPrices). Expose via POST endpoint. XIRR must NOT use post-CA adjusted prices from TradeOutcomeEntity — only raw TransactionEntity cash flows.

steps.description: Create XirrCalculator utility with Newton-Raphson, NPV, derivative, convergence, and fallback logic
Implement cash flow construction from TransactionEntity in AnalyticsService
Support optional currentPrices in request body to include open positions as notional cash flow

phases.name: Tests
phases.description: Add comprehensive unit tests for AnalyticsService (mocked repositories) and XirrCalculator (pure math). Add integration tests with Testcontainers MongoDB seeding realistic data including corporate actions and multipart sells, then assert all endpoints. Seed TradeOutcomeEntity via the sellStock() write path.

steps.description: Add AnalyticsService unit tests covering all methods and edge cases
Add XirrCalculator unit tests including non-convergent and edge cases
Add AnalyticsIntegrationTest seeding data and calling all endpoints

decisions.title: Work on feature branch
decisions.description: All Phase 1 work (and subsequent phases) must be committed to a feature branch, not master. Need to create branch and ensure all edits are committed there.

XIRR Calculator matches Excel/Google Sheets XIRR for known test cases within 0.01% tolerance
AnalyticsService unit tests pass for all 4 features
AnalyticsIntegrationTest passes with seeded data including corporate actions and multipart sells
